refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: platformd-secretd.service is a user service that provides the freedesktop.org Secret Service API on the session bus. It is an implementation of org.freedesktop.secrets whose purpose is to bind the release of a stored secret to the platform-authentication state of the session — recent user verification, lock and unlock, and the identity of the calling process — rather than to bus access alone.

Applications reach it through the standard Secret Service API, so unmodified clients using libsecret — including secret-tool1 — work without change. The provider is inspected and managed with secretctl1.

The service claims the well-known bus name org.freedesktop.secrets only when it is the active provider for the session; it does not import or evict another provider automatically. Only one Secret Service provider may own that name at a time. The daemon announces readiness through sd_notify3 and shuts down cleanly on SIGTERM.

refsect1.title: The Secret Service interface
refsect1.para: The service exposes the standard object hierarchy under /org/freedesktop/secrets: the org.freedesktop.Secret.Service entry point, a default org.freedesktop.Secret.Collection (also reachable through the aliases/default path, where libsecret looks for it), per-secret org.freedesktop.Secret.Item objects, and transport org.freedesktop.Secret.Session objects. At present only the plain session transport is implemented; clients that request the encrypted transport fall back to it.
For the full interface, the storage model, and the trust model, see the reference documentation shipped with the source, docs/secret-service.md.

refsect1.title: Security
refsect1.para: The provider currently keeps secrets in memory and in plaintext, and makes no security claim. Encryption at rest and authentication-bound release are in development. See docs/secret-service.md for the threat model and the limits of what the provider protects.
